import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

import { DefaultTheme } from 'styled-components'

declare module 'styled-components' {
  export interface DefaultTheme {
    name: 'light' | 'dark'
    fonts: {
      family: string
    }
    spacing: typeof spacing
    color: typeof color
    borderRadius: typeof borderRadius
    boxShadow: typeof boxShadow
    typography: typeof typography

}

  id: number
  name: string
  price: number
  imageUrl?: string
  description?: string
}

  name: string
  description?: string
  price: number
}

    food: FoodMenuItem[]

}

transcript.text: The app wraps pages in `BrowserRouter`, Redux `Provider`, styled-components `ThemeProvider`, and `GlobalStyle`. It also needs a `#modal` portal target. Axios calls are centralized at the Mealdrop Netlify function URL, so MSW can target that specific endpoint.

transcript.aggregated_output:
import styled from 'styled-components'

import { PageTemplate } from '../../templates/PageTemplate'
import { categories } from '../../stub/categories'

import { RestaurantsSection } from './components/RestaurantsSection'
import { CategoriesSection } from './components/CategoriesSection/CategoriesSection'
import { Banner } from './components/Banner'
import { AwardWinningSection } from './components/AwardWinningSection'

const Spacing = styled.div`
  margin-bottom: 4.5rem;
`

export const HomePage = () => (
  <PageTemplate>
    <Banner />
    <Spacing />
    <RestaurantsSection title="Our favorite picks" />
    <Spacing />
    <AwardWinningSection />
    <Spacing />
    <CategoriesSection categories={categories} />
    <Spacing />
  </PageTemplate>
)

{
  "compilerOptions": {

}

transcript.text: The first batch found one real portal nuance: `Modal` only renders after a state change because its `Portal` stores the target in a ref without forcing a render. I’m keeping the component as-is and changing only the Modal story to open it through a small trigger interaction, which matches how the app usually changes `isOpen`.

import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

content.text: The app wraps pages in `BrowserRouter`, Redux `Provider`, styled-components `ThemeProvider`, and `GlobalStyle`. It also needs a `#modal` portal target. Axios calls are centralized at the Mealdrop Netlify function URL, so MSW can target that specific endpoint.

import styled from 'styled-components'

import { PageTemplate } from '../../templates/PageTemplate'
import { categories } from '../../stub/categories'

import { RestaurantsSection } from './components/RestaurantsSection'
import { CategoriesSection } from './components/CategoriesSection/CategoriesSection'
import { Banner } from './components/Banner'
import { AwardWinningSection } from './components/AwardWinningSection'

const Spacing = styled.div`
  margin-bottom: 4.5rem;
`

export const HomePage = () => (
  <PageTemplate>
    <Banner />
    <Spacing />
    <RestaurantsSection title="Our favorite picks" />
    <Spacing />
    <AwardWinningSection />
    <Spacing />
    <CategoriesSection categories={categories} />
    <Spacing />
  </PageTemplate>
)

content.text: The story targets are clear now: a small set of reusable components plus the restaurant list/detail UI that exercises routing, Redux, portals, styled-components, carousel CSS, and API data. I’m going to add the shared preview/MSW setup first, then create the stories in one batch.

content.text: The app has no date-dependent render output, so `mockdate` would be an unused dev dependency after setup. I’m removing that one cleanup dependency and leaving `msw` plus `msw-storybook-addon`, which are used by the preview and handlers.
